findings.evidence: `rg -n "tailFamily_le|measurable_tailFamily|tailFamily_eq" code/TauCeti/Probability/Process/Tail.lean` shows only `tailFamily_eq_iSup_comap`, `measurable_tailFamily_of_le`, and the ambient-specialized `tailFamily_le_ambient`, but no general `tailFamily_le_iff`/minimality lemma.
findings.fix: Add a public universal-property lemma such as `tailFamily_le_iff` and derive `tailFamily_le_ambient` from it, so users can reason against arbitrary sub-sigma-algebras without unfolding `tailFamily`.
findings.issue: `tailFamily` only has the specialized `tailFamily_le_ambient`; there is no general characteristic lemma for the generated sigma-algebra, e.g. `tailFamily X n ≤ m ↔ ∀ k, n ≤ k → Measurable[m] (X k)`.

summary: The new surface names the generated future sigma-algebras but does not expose their universal property, so downstream users must unfold the definition to prove measurability/minimality against an arbitrary sigma-algebra.
